meta.title: mal

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: mal
root.mentor_note: mal is bene’s opposite number, and every word it builds carries that same shadow.

attempt.direction_prompt: A meal that nourished her, or one that made her ill?

context_sentences: Doctors treating the region saw childhood malnutrition drop sharply once the new clinics opened.
Years of malnutrition had left visible marks that better meals alone could not fully reverse.
The scan revealed a small malignant growth that surgery caught early enough to remove entirely.
What had looked benign under the first microscope turned out, on closer study, to be malignant.
No specialist could name the malady, though three separate ones had tried over two years.
The old sailor blamed every malady, real or imagined, on a lifetime spent at sea.

construct_options.text: An illness or disease, especially a chronic or hard to diagnose one
A lack of proper nutrition from not eating enough good food
A state of excellent, robust health

context_sentences: The old folk tale ends with a malediction that follows the family for three generations.
Villagers still avoided the ruined house, half remembering some malediction laid on it long ago.

construct_options.text: A curse, a spoken wish for harm to fall on someone
A blessing spoken to bring good fortune
A chronic illness that resists diagnosis
